name: pyparlaclarin

metadata.name: pyparlaclarin
metadata.summary: Read, create, and modify Parla-Clarin XML files

documentation:
# Pyparlaclarin

This module includes functionality for reading, creating, and modifying Parla-Clarin XML files.

For instance, you can loop over all paragraphs in a Parla-Clarin file with a simple function:

```python
from pyparlaclarin.read import paragraph_iterator

for paragraph in paragraph_iterator(root):
	print(paragraph)
```

or get all speeches by a speaker

```python
from pyparlaclarin.read import speeches_with_name

for speech in speeches_with_name(root, name="barack_obama_1961"):
	print(speech)
```

Further documentation is available on [GitHub pages](https://welfare-state-analytics.github.io/pyparlaclarin/pyparlaclarin/).

modules.name: pyparlaclarin
documentation: Read, create, and modify Parla-Clarin XML files

modules.name: pyparlaclarin.create
documentation: Generate new Parla Clarin documents

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.create.pc_header
documentation:
Generate a teiHeader for Parla-Clarin documents.

Args:
    metadata: python dictionary with the appropriate metadata. Missing keys
        are allowed and filled with 'N/A' values.
Generate parla clarin header

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.create.create_tei
documentation:
Create a Parla-Clarin TEI element from a list of segments.

Args:
    txts: a list of lists of strings, corresponds to content blocks and paragraphs, respectively.
    metadata: Metadata of the parliamentary session

modules.name: pyparlaclarin.refine
documentation: Modify and curate Parla-Clarin documents

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.refine.reclassify
documentation:
Reclassify nodes in a Parla-Clarin tree.

Args:
    root: root of the lxml tree to be reclassified
    classifier: lambda function that classifies paragraphs. lxml.elem->str,
        takes paragraph as input, outputs predicted xml tag, such
        as note or u.
    tei: namespace for the output xml
    exclude: exclude certain tags or types of element from reclassification

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.refine.format_paragraph
documentation:
Formats paragraphs to be equal in width.

Args:
    paragraph: paragraph content, str.
    spaces: size of indentation as number of spaces.

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.refine.format_texts
documentation:
Formats all text elements in a Parla-Clarin document.

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document as an lxml tree root.

modules.name: pyparlaclarin.read
documentation: Read and extract information from Parla-Clarin documents.
!/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.element_hash
documentation: Calculate a deterministic hash for an XML element

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.parlaclarin_to_md
documentation:
Convert Parla-Clarin XML to markdown. Returns a string.

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document root, as an lxml tree root.

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.parlaclarin_to_txt
documentation:
Convert Parla-Clarin XML to plain text. Returns a string.

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document root, as an lxml tree root.

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.speeches_with_name
documentation:
Convert Parla-Clarin XML to plain text. Returns a concatenated string.

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document root, as an lxml tree root. 
    name: Name of the person whose speeches are returned. If name is None, returns all speeches.
    return_ids: Return a generator of tuples (text, id) instead of only text. Default False.

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.speech_iterator
documentation:
Convert Parla-Clarin XML to an iterator of of concatenated speeches and speaker ids.
Speech segments are concatenated unless a new speaker appears (ignoring any notes).

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document root, as an lxml tree root.

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.paragraph_iterator
documentation:
Convert Parla-Clarin XML to an iterator of paragraphs. 

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document root, as an lxml tree root.
    output: Output format of paragraphs. Accepts "str" (default), or "lxml".

functions.name: pyparlaclarin.read.get_dates
documentation:
Get dates of a Parla-Clarin file. 

Args:
    root: Parla-Clarin document root, as an lxml tree root.

returns:
    as a datetime
